Project Tipping Point – by Erin Remblance, Ryan (Ra) James Kemp and Kasper Benjamin Reimer Bjørkskov
note: To learn about social tipping points, and how to create a more beautiful world rooted in authentic regenerative, beautiful, post-growth futures

title: 2. Sustainability as the World Needs – by Alice Kalro and the team at arkH3
note: To learn about what sustainability truly means, and how we can embrace true sustainability for businesses.

title: 3. Climate Champion Course – by Dan Sherrard-Smith and MotherTree
note: To learn how to take the most impactful actions to reduce our carbon emissions and make a positive impact, from finances and beyond.

title: 4. The Bold Training – by Dan Pallotta
note: To learn more about how to transform the non-profit and charity sector, to empower people to create even more social impact at scale.

title: 5. The Carbon Literacy Project training
note: To learn more about carbon emissions and how to minimize them in different sectors and environments.

title: 6. Ashoka Systems Change course
note: To learn more about systems change and how we can apply systems thinking to bring it all together and ensure I’m taking a holistic approach to creating better systems.

title: 7. Social Impact course on Coursera, recommended by my friend Nipuna.
note: To learn more about how to create social impact, how to measure impact and how to progress as a social entrepreneur.

title: 8. Impact MBA – by Tomorrow University of Applied Sciences (this one might be tricky to fit it!), thanks to their amazing team including Julia Rosner, Dr. Thomas Funke and Christian Rebernik.
